source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The normal human eye has maximum visual acuity with a pupil diameter of about 3\ \mathrm{mm}. For larger pupils, acuity decreases due to increasing aberrations; for smaller pupils, acuity decreases due to increasing diffraction. If your pupil diameter is 2.0\ \mathrm{mm}, as it would be in bright light, what is the smallest-diameter circle that you should be able to see as a circle, rather than just an unresolved blob, on an eye chart at the standard distance of 20\ \mathrm{ft}?
